key_points: A polynomial is an algebraic expression made up of variables and coefficients
The power of the variable in a polynomial is always a whole number
The highest power of the variable is called the degree of the polynomial
Polynomials are classified as linear, quadratic, cubic, etc., based on degree
Zeroes of a polynomial are the values of x for which the polynomial becomes zero
formulas: Zeroes of p(x) are values of x for which p(x) = 0
exam_tips: The number of zeroes of a polynomial is at most equal to its degree
Zeroes of a polynomial are the x-intercepts of its graph
Degree of a constant polynomial is 0
Degree of zero polynomial is not defined
